title: World report on ageing and health
abstract: Reframes healthy ageing around functional ability and intrinsic capacity, and argues for age-friendly environments and integrated, person-centred long-term care systems.

title: Dementia in Canada
abstract: Describes the prevalence of dementia in Canada, use of home care and long-term care by people living with dementia, caregiver burden, and avoidable acute-care use.

title: Evidence informing A Dementia Strategy for Canada
abstract: Summarizes evidence behind Canada's national dementia strategy: prevention, therapy and cure, and improving quality of life for people living with dementia and their caregivers.

title: Home care and avoidable emergency department use among older adults: a review
abstract: Reviews evidence on whether expanded home care is associated with reduced avoidable emergency department visits and hospitalizations among older adults, noting heterogeneity and confounding challenges.

title: Aging in place: outcomes of community-based supports for older adults
abstract: Examines whether home and community care supports help older adults remain in their homes longer and the effect on independence, social participation, and care costs.

title: Public reporting and primary care access in Nova Scotia
authors: Nova Scotia Health / Department of Health and Wellness
abstract: Describes Nova Scotia's Action for Health measures and public dashboard, including the Need a Family Practice Registry and expanded pharmacy and urgent-care primary-care access.

title: Canada's population estimates: age and sex
abstract: Reports July 1 population estimates by age and gender, documenting the growth of the population aged 65 and over in Canada and the provinces, the denominator for per-capita aging indicators.
